questions.question: Derive the update rule for Adagrad optimization algorithm, where the learning rate for each parameter is adaptively adjusted based on the square of the magnitude of the gradient. Consider a single parameter update rule. Assume the learning rate is denoted as η and the parameter value is denoted as x.
questions.hint: Use mathematical derivations to support your answer.

questions.question: Prove that Adagrad optimization algorithm has a non-zero minimum, i.e., the optimal solution does not converge to the zero vector. Provide a counterexample to demonstrate this.
questions.hint: Use mathematical proofs to support your answer.

questions.question: Consider a simple neural network with a single hidden layer consisting of 10 neurons, each with ReLU activation. The weights are initialized randomly from a normal distribution with mean 0 and standard deviation 1. The loss function is the mean squared error (MSE). Derive the gradient of the loss function with respect to the weights and then apply Adagrad optimization algorithm to update the weights. Assume the learning rate is 0.01.
questions.hint: Be sure to show all the mathematical derivations and provide a clear explanation of the Adagrad update rule.
